step_by_step_reasoning: We identify the system as Singtel Electronic Mobile Top-up (EMTU). External mobile and merchant users connect via the Internet Gateway (IGW) to the Application Load Balancer (ALB). Internal Singtel support and management users connect through a Virtual Private Gateway (VGW) to the same ALB. The ALB routes traffic down to a multi-tiered highly available architecture: Web tier (EC2), App tier (EC2), and a database tier (Amazon Aurora). The whiteboard contains two duplicate stacks for high availability, which we map to single logical tiers for simplicity and clarity.

graph.name: Singtel Electronic Mobile Top-up System
graph.notes: Electronic Mobile Top-up system segregating internal/external traffic via IGW/VGW, using ALB to direct traffic to highly-available multi-tier EC2 stacks, and Aurora as the system of record.

nodes.name: Merchants & Mobile Users
nodes.notes: External consumers initiating mobile top-up transactions.

nodes.name: Singtel Support & Management
nodes.notes: Internal staff managing application and analyzing performance.

nodes.name: Application Load Balancer
nodes.notes: Distributes traffic and handles web front-end failover.

nodes.name: Web Servers
nodes.notes: Web front-end servers for user interface.

nodes.name: Amazon Aurora
nodes.notes: Relational database used as the secure system of record.

edges.notes: Routes external traffic to load balancer.
Routes internal traffic to load balancer.
ALB directs traffic to the active web instance.
Web tier communicates with App tier.
App tier queries and updates database.
